blocks.content: In addition to managed observability databases, every Grafana Cloud stack includes a set of core services. These components work together so you can monitor your systems, respond to issues, and validate reliability from a single platform.
Open the main menu on the left side of the screen.
Click **Dashboards**.
This is where you build and view visualizations for data you have sent to Grafana Cloud or connected from external sources. Notice there are a few prebuilt dashboards available.
Click **Alerts & IRM**.

Here you can see the options for:

- **Alerting** to create and view alert rules, contact points, and notification policies
- **Service center**, a unified view of your services with operational context attached
- **IRM**, for on-call schedules, escalation policies, and incident coordination
- **SLO**, to create Service Level Objectives
- **Labels**, to create and manage labels for alerts, incidents, and SLOs
Click **Testing & synthetics**.
Here you find Synthetic Monitoring and k6 performance testing. These tools let you proactively validate availability and performance before users are affected.
Click **Drilldown**.
With Drilldown you can explore, filter, and investigate metrics, logs, traces, and profiles without knowing a query language.
Click **Explore**.
Explore lets you write and run queries directly against any connected data source. Unlike Drilldown, you need to know a query language such as PromQL or LogQL to use it.
Click **AI & machine learning**.

These built-in tools let you:

- Forecast and detect anomalies
- Detect outliers
- Run automatic investigations to find unusual system activity

These tools are also available as part of some observability solutions, including Kubernetes Monitoring.
Click **Adaptive Telemetry**.

Adaptive Telemetry helps you control costs by identifying data you're not using and filtering out low-value data. It works across metrics, logs, traces, and profiles so you only pay for the data that matters.
Click **Cost Management and Billing**.
Here you monitor how much you're using and spending, view invoices, and set up alerts that notify you when usage or costs approach a defined limit.
For a full overview of every capability covered in this milestone and more, refer to the [Introduction to Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/introduction/).
In the next milestone, you explore dashboard options for visualizing your data.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your path, you can explore the following topics:

- [Alerts and IRM](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/alerting-and-irm/)
- [Testing and synthetics](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/)
- [Drilldown apps](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/visualizations/simplified-exploration/)
- [Explore](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/visualizations/explore/)
- [AI and machine learning](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/machine-learning/)
- [Adaptive Telemetry](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/adaptive-telemetry/)
- [Cost Management and Billing](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/cost-management-and-billing/)
